topics: California housing policy and CEQA reform
Homelessness prevention and interim housing strategies
State-city relations and fiscal incentives for housing
California Forever and master-planned community development
Mental health, coercion, and Housing First accountability

claims.claim: Litigation as a housing enforcement tool is not particularly effective at actually getting housing built
evidence: Huntington Beach is not building more housing despite legal pressure
A San Diego County project with 2,000 units sued under AG Becerra still hasn't been built

claims.claim: Measure ULA caused an 84% drop in construction in Los Angeles according to a RAND/UCLA study
evidence: ULA is a transfer tax on properties over $5 million including multifamily and commercial, not just mansions
RAND and UCLA study cited showing 84% construction drop

claims.claim: California's $24 billion spent on homelessness from 2018 to 2023 failed because outcomes were not tracked and accountability was absent
evidence: $24 billion spent mostly within Housing First framework
Unsheltered population continued to grow
Becerra: 'We didn't focus on outcomes'

claims.claim: Only one in seven people who becomes homeless has a pre-existing mental health problem, but virtually everyone who stays on the street long-term develops one
evidence: Being homeless is described as 'incredibly stressful, vulnerable and dangerous'
Steyer uses this to argue for rapid removal from streets as a mental health intervention

claims.claim: Direct cash assistance is the most effective and cost-efficient way to prevent eviction and foreclosure
evidence: Median cost of cash-based eviction/foreclosure prevention is $6,000 per family
Compare to $750,000–$1 million per unit for permanent assisted housing
Audacious Foundation funded pilots across California

claims.claim: Emergency interim housing—with private rooms, keys, pet allowances, and wraparound services—gets approximately 70% of unsheltered people off the street, far more than traditional shelters
evidence: Traditional shelters seen as dangerous, no privacy, no pets allowed
Steyer claims emergency interim housing acceptance rate around 70%
Permanent assisted housing costs $750,000–$1 million per key

claims.claim: San Jose's prevention model, partnered with Destination Home, kept over 92% of assisted households housed without ongoing public subsidy
evidence: Notre Dame University longitudinal study over 3–4 years
One-time rental assistance paired with case management
San Jose reduced inflow by 50% from streets
speakers: Mayor Mayhem (Mayor Matt Mahan of San Jose)

claims.claim: CEQA is broken because, unlike NEPA, it does not require environmental standing to sue, allowing anyone from anywhere to sue California projects
evidence: 'You can sue from Richmond, Virginia for a project in Richmond, California'
Stone Ranch project had 33 CEQA objections; after resolving 29, lost on one and had to restart all 33

quote_candidates.text: If you want to be popular, get a dog.
quote_candidates.speaker: Mr. Villaraigosa (Antonio Villaraigosa)

quote_candidates.text: Only one in seven people who becomes homeless has a mental health problem. But virtually everyone who stays on the street for a long time develops one.
quote_candidates.speaker: Mr. Steyer (Tom Steyer)

quote_candidates.text: You can sue from Richmond, Virginia for a project in Richmond, California. Yes, you can. It's broken.
quote_candidates.speaker: Mr. Villaraigosa (Antonio Villaraigosa)

quote_candidates.text: The opposite perception that we can't trust people with cash, we can't give low income people cash is racist. It's sexist and it's wrong. And it costs us a fortune.
quote_candidates.speaker: Ms. Porter (Katie Porter)

quote_candidates.text: We didn't focus on outcomes. There was the accountability wasn't there. Twenty four billion dollars was there, but the outcomes didn't result.
quote_candidates.speaker: Mr. Becerra (Xavier Becerra)

quote_candidates.text: No one gets well on the street.
quote_candidates.speaker: Mr. Steyer (Tom Steyer)

guest_candidates.name: Antonio Villaraigosa
guest_candidates.title: Former Mayor of Los Angeles, California Governor Candidate

guest_candidates.name: Xavier Becerra
guest_candidates.title: Former California Attorney General and U.S. Secretary of HHS, Governor Candidate

guest_candidates.name: Tom Steyer
guest_candidates.title: Businessman and Activist, California Governor Candidate

guest_candidates.name: Katie Porter
guest_candidates.title: Former U.S. Representative, California Governor Candidate

guest_candidates.name: Matt Mahan
guest_candidates.title: Mayor of San Jose, California Governor Candidate
